commit.message:
Fix npm detection: use exit code instead of output string

The previous logic checked if "npm" appeared in output, but error messages
like "which: npm: Command not found" also contain "npm", causing false
positives. Now uses the exit code from `which` directly.

commit.message:
fix: run inline_scope_output_names before compact_temp_names (+2 correct)

Previously compact_temp_names could reuse _T0 (a function parameter slot)
for _T1 (a scope-output temp) when their live ranges didn't overlap. Then
inline_scope_output_names would see `const z = _T0;` and incorrectly
rename the parameter _T0 to z everywhere, including the function signature.

Fix: run inline_scope_output_names first so the scope-output temp is
replaced with its real name before compaction, preventing slot conflicts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
server: add cron scheduling for applications (#2219)

* server: add cron scheduling for applications

Adds support for recurring cron-based invocations of applications. Users
declare a cron expression on the application (e.g. "*/5 * * * *") and the
server automatically fires InvokeApplication requests on schedule.

Key changes:
- CronScheduleEntry data model + CronSchedules column family in RocksDB
- CronProcessor with DelayQueue-based timer, watch channel for reload
- Cron invocations use no-arg entrypoints (empty inputs, no blob store writes)
- Cron expression validated at the HTTP API boundary (400 on invalid)
- GET application API returns next_cron_fire_time_ms when cron is set
- invoke_helper.rs extracts shared invocation logic from the HTTP route
- Conditional cron notifications to avoid unnecessary CF scans
- write_mutex serialization in reschedule_after_fire
- 13 integration tests covering CRUD lifecycle, watch channel, multi-app

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* server: use targeted cron events instead of full CF scan

Replace the watch<()> channel with mpsc::unbounded<CronEvent> carrying
namespace + app name. The CronProcessor now does point reads on event
receipt instead of scanning the entire CronSchedules CF. Full CF scan
is only performed once at startup.

Events are batched (drain pending) and deduplicated by (namespace, app)
before processing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* server: fix clippy and fmt warnings

Collapse nested if statements per clippy::collapsible_if, apply nightly
rustfmt formatting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* server: apply nightly rustfmt formatting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ink-bleed scroll reveals, animated quill dividers, torn-edge work cards,
wax-seal buttons, and a letter-style contact form.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add CRDT collaborative editing via Yjs Y.Text per scene

Each scene's text content is now backed by a Yjs Y.Text CRDT, enabling
true real-time collaborative editing where multiple users can type
simultaneously without conflicts. Edits merge automatically at the
character level via the Yjs sync protocol over WebSocket.

Key changes:
- SceneTextBox now binds to Y.Text instead of broadcasting full text blobs
- Local input: computes minimal diff (common prefix/suffix) and applies
  insert/delete operations to Y.Text
- Remote changes: Y.Text observer rebuilds DOM with cursor preservation
  using div-index + offset tracking
- Y.Text initialized from DB on first sync (first user populates it)
- DB persistence on blur for durability (Y.Text is source of truth)
- Undo updates Y.Text so changes propagate to all clients
- Added helper functions: textToHtml, getRawEditorText, computeTextDiff,
  saveDivCursorState, restoreDivCursorState, getCursorTextOffset,
  setCursorTextOffset
- useYjs: added getSceneText() helper
- page.tsx: passes yDoc and synced props to ScriptView
- Removed handleSceneTyping (Y.Text handles sync natively)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add dev harness enforcement: hooks + artifacts enforce dev-domain stages

Extracts 13 enforceable steps from domains/dev.md into dev/tasks.json
with verify_cmd for each. Pre-commit hook (verify-harness.js) rejects
commits where tasks are flipped to pass without verification proof and
progress.md log entry. Router injection in dev.md surfaces harness
state on every dev domain entry.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: validate database schema in golden contract tests (#26)

The golden contract tests previously checked only whether database.db
existed (fs.existsSync), but an empty SQLite stub file (8192 bytes)
also passes that check. This caused CI failures with "no such table:
legal_documents" errors.

Now the guard also validates that the database contains the expected
`legal_documents` table before running tests. If the database is
missing, empty, or corrupt (e.g. a Git LFS pointer), the contract
tests are cleanly skipped.

Co-authored-by: Jeffrey von Rotz <jeffrey.von.rotz@ansvar.eu>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
